creator: marklmi26-fbsd@yahoo.com
text:
(In reply to Mark Millard from comment #2)

Note that if FreeBSD specific code is isolated via the preprocessor directives it can be acceptable upstream. There already is such code in places for various Operating Systems.

But code that is used for an open-ended range of operating systems, compilers, etc. that is to go upstream needs to be C89/C90 based (other than use of long long and %lld and the like that was fairly common a few years after C89/C90) yet C99/C11 compatible.
